source_note: EIA (2024): Saudi Arabia = 38% of Hormuz crude+condensate, 5.5 mb/d. East-West nameplate 5.0 mb/d, pushed toward 7.0 during the crisis by converting NGL lines (S&P Global, 10 Mar 2026). Nameplate-optimistic spare 3.5; EIA puts REAL combined KSA+UAE deliverable spare at only 2.6 mb/d.

Share is an ESTIMATE: EIA names only Saudi Arabia's share; non-Saudi splits approximate 2024 Gulf export volumes and are not individually published. No Gulf-connected bypass (Kirkuk-Ceyhan runs to the Mediterranean).

EIA: ADCOP/Fujairah nameplate 1.8 mb/d; EIA calls real excess capacity only 'limited' (no figure). Share is an ESTIMATE. The 1.4 nameplate-optimistic spare is the naive infrastructure read the model deliberately tests.
Share is an ESTIMATE. Goreh-Jask bypass ~0.3 mb/d nameplate but ran <0.07 mb/d in 2024 (EIA); treated as ~0 deliverable. Iran retained strait access as the controlling party.
Share is an ESTIMATE (see Iraq note). No bypass infrastructure.
Share is an ESTIMATE (see Iraq note). Crude/condensate only; QatarEnergy declared LNG force majeure separately (LNG not modeled here).
